item.text: I’m going to scan `crates/orbit-engine/src/` for direct `.run_deterministic(...)` call sites, then map each hit to its enclosing `fn` with focused file reads so every item is tool-verified.
I found five raw call hits. Next I’m inspecting `groundhog.rs` around each line to confirm whether they’re in production functions (not test-only code) and extract exact containing `fn` names.

I’ve confirmed the groundhog hits are in normal top-level functions (no in-file test module markers). I’ll run one broader regex pass to catch spaced variants and make sure we didn’t miss any call style.

item.command: /bin/zsh -lc "cd /Users/daniel/workspace/repos/orbit && rg -n \"\\.run_deterministic\\s*\\(\" crates/orbit-engine/src | while IFS=: read -r path line rest; do fn="'$(awk -v target="$line" '"'NR<=target { if ("'$0 ~ /''^[[:space:]]*fn[[:space:]]+[A-Za-z0-9_]+[[:space:]]*'"\\(/) { match("'$0,/fn[[:space:]]+([A-Za-z0-9_]+)/,m); name=m[1]; } } END { print name }'"' \""'$path"); printf "%s:%s -> %s'"\\n\" \""'$path" "$line" "$fn"; done'

/bin/zsh -lc "cd /Users/daniel/workspace/repos/orbit && rg -n \"\\.run_deterministic\\s*\\(\" crates/orbit-engine/src | while IFS=: read -r path line rest; do fn="'$(awk -v target="$line" '"'NR<=target { if ("'$0 ~ /''^[[:space:]]*fn[[:space:]]+[A-Za-z0-9_]+[[:space:]]*'"\\(/) { match("'$0,/fn[[:space:]]+([A-Za-z0-9_]+)/,m); name=m[1]; } } END { print name }'"' \""'$path"); printf "%s:%s -> %s'"\\n\" \""'$path" "$line" "$fn"; done'
